name: Apple of Discord
description: The Apple of Discord, also known as the Apple of Eris, is a golden apple inscribed with the word "Kallisti" (Ancient Greek: καλλίστῃ), meaning "For the fairest". It was thrown into the wedding banquet of Peleus and Thetis by Eris, the goddess of discord, who was not invited due to her disruptive nature. Her act was intended to cause chaos among the goddesses present, specifically Hera, Athena, and Aphrodite, each of whom believed themselves to be the fairest. This rivalry led to a heated argument and a request for Zeus to judge the matter. Unwilling to take sides himself, Zeus appointed Paris, a Trojan prince known for his fair judgment, to decide. Each goddess attempted to bribe Paris, with Aphrodite promising him the most beautiful woman in the world, Helen of Sparta. Paris chose Aphrodite, awarding her the apple and setting in motion the events that would ultimately lead to the Trojan War. The Apple of Discord represents the destructive power of envy, vanity, and unchecked ambition. It highlights how a seemingly small act of discord can have catastrophic consequences, affecting not only individuals but entire civilizations. The apple itself is a symbol of temptation and the allure of power, demonstrating how the pursuit of personal gain can override considerations of morality and peace. The story serves as a cautionary tale about the importance of humility, diplomacy, and the dangers of succumbing to the allure of selfish desires.
subtitle: The Seed of the Trojan War
origin: Crafted by Eris, Goddess of Discord

keyMyths.title: The Wedding of Peleus and Thetis
keyMyths.description: Eris, uninvited to the wedding, throws the Apple of Discord, sparking a conflict among the goddesses. This act sets the stage for the Judgement of Paris and the Trojan War.

keyMyths.title: The Judgement of Paris
keyMyths.description: Paris chooses Aphrodite as the fairest, awarding her the apple and earning her favor. His reward is Helen, wife of Menelaus, leading to the abduction and the start of the Trojan War.

sources.author: Homer
sources.description: Provides context for the Trojan War, indirectly linked to the Apple of Discord.
sources.text: Provides context for the Trojan War, indirectly linked to the Apple of Discord.

sources.author: Apollodorus
sources.description: Offers a comprehensive account of Greek mythology, including the story of the Apple of Discord.
